name: Brave
description: Free, open-source Chromium-based web browser developed by Brave Software with built-in ad and tracker blocking.

years.notes: Lion-in-shield mark by Jimmy Haas, unveiled October 30, 2017 for the Brave 0.18 release. Two-stop orange-to-red gradient (#FF5500 → #FF2000) over a white inset face. Carried Brave through the 1.0 stable release in November 2019 and remains the canonical icon mark.

Subtle refresh shipped with the 2024 brand refresh that introduced the Poppins Semibold wordmark. The lion silhouette is geometrically unchanged from 2017; the gradient was tuned to three stops (#FF5601 → #FF4000 → #FF1F01) for a slightly warmer mid-tone. Current mark used across brave.com and Brave Search.

notes: Skipped millésimes: 2016 launch logo (first beta, January 2016 — no authoritative vector survives; only low-res raster references prior to the October 2017 Jimmy Haas redesign). Wordmark/typography changed in 2024 but the icon mark only saw a minor gradient tuning, hence two millésimes rather than three.
